title: Cyste montana
intro: Cyste Montana, also known as Marsh Marigold or Caltha palustris, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyste Montana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyste Montana is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and bronchitis.
